content.full_text:
Administrative Offices
91 ASHFIELD ROAD
ATHERTON, CALIFORNIA 94027-3896
(415) 325-4457
FAX (415) 688-6528

FPPC
JAN
December 27, 1990

The Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804

Dear Sirs,

First, I would like to thank you for your informative seminar you held in Redwood City on December 13, 1990. It was very helpful to me. Possibly more than you could realize since I was newly appointed Deputy City Clerk and given the responsibility of handling the forms for your department. The reason for this letter is to hopefully straighten out any past problems or misfilings from our Town. Since I have been given sole responsibility of this, I need your help in advising me just exactly who needs to file and which form is to be filed for them, so that there are no further misfilings. I have enclosed your form for our receiving any updates on amended standardized Conflict of Interest Code, along with a copy of our Resolution 80-30, which was adopted June 24, 1980 stating which officials need to file and a list of officials with the Town of Atherton. I would also like to know how I would obtain in a copy of the Political Reform Act. I do understand that the new officials who assumed office in November and December 1990 do not file an annual statement until January 1992 and I have advised them of this. I also understand that in January I will be receiving forms for the other elected and appointed officials and need to be sure they file the correct form.

If possible, please advise me on this matter. It would be most appreciated. I need to be clear on this matter and need this information confirmed. If you need to contact me, my direct line is 415-688-6529.

Sincerely,

Sue Jankowski
Deputy City Clerk

ENCLOSURES

STANDARDIZED CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODES


ALL CITIES, COUNTIES OR LOCAL AGENCIES which have adopted the
standardized conflict of interest code (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18730) need to let us know so that we can keep them up to
date on amendments to the code. Please take a minute to complete
this form and return it to:

                    Fair Political Practices Commission
                    ATTN: Legal Division
                    P.O. Box 807
                    Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -

The conflict of interest codes of the following cities, counties
and/or local agencies now incorporate by reference the terms of 2
Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18730:

__________________________________________________________________

__________________________________________________________________

__________________________________________________________________


Proposed amendments to this regulation should be sent to the
following address:

     Town of Atherton

     Attn: Sue Jankowski - Deputy City Clerk

     91 Ashfield Road

     Atherton, CA.  94027


                                                           1/90

RESOLUTION NO. 80-30
RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE TOWN OF ATHERTON
TO AMEND CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE FOR CITY OFFICIALS


WHEREAS, AMENDMENTS TO Political Reform Act, Government Code
Sections 81000, et seq. have in the past and foreseeably will in the future
require conforming amendments to be made in Conflict of Interest Codes adopted
and promulgated pursuant to its provisions; and

WHEREAS, the Fair Political Practices Commission has adopted a
regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730, which contains the terms of a
standard model Conflict of Interest Code, which can be incorporated by refer-
ence, and which will be amended to conform to amendments in the Political
Reform Act after public notice and hearings conducted by the Fair Political
Practices Commission pursuant to the Administrative Procedure Act, Government
Code Section 11370, et seq.; and

WHEREAS, incorporation by reference of the terms of the afore-
mentioned regulation and amendments to it in Conflict of Interest Codes will
save this body time and money by minimizing the actions required of this body
to keep the Codes in conformity with the Political Reform Act;

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the City Council of the Town of
Atherton as follows:

1. All previously adopted resolutions approving various separate
Conflict of Interest Codes are hereby rescinded.

2. The terms of 2 Cal.Adm. Code Section 18730 and any amendments
to it duly adopted by the Fair Political Practices Commission, along with the
attached Appendices in which officials and employees are designated and dis-
closure categories are set forth, are hereby incorporated by reference and
constitute the Conflict of Interest Codes for the following departments and
agencies:
   CITY MANAGER'S OFFICE                    POLICE DEPARTMENT

   City Manager                             Chief of Police
                                            Captain of Police

   BUILDING DEPARTMENT                      CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE

   Building Inspector                       City Attorney

OFFICIALS CURRENTLY HOLDING OFFICE

CITY COUNCIL

Christopher E. Cobey
William R. Conwell
Malcolm H. Dudley
Nanette F. Chapman
Dianne M. Fisher

PLANNING COMMISSION

Charles J. Abbe
Charles De Maria
Kent Kaiser
James Janz
Marion E. Oster

PARK AND RECREATION COMMISSION

Charles Horton
Henry Cartan
Susan Brady
John Estes
Robert McAfee

CITY OFFICIALS

Richard L. Moore (City Manager)
Dennis R. Lahr (Finance Director)
Donald McPherson (Building Official)

Ronald A. Harris (Police Captain)
Patrick N. Rolle (Police Chief)

Robin Faisant (City Attorney)
Gordon Tillson (City Engineer)

INDEX OF STATEMENTS SENT 1990

ASSUMED OFFICE:                          STATEMENT SENT:

Dianne M. Fisher      November 27, 1990      December 7, 1990

Marion E. Oster       December 19, 1990      December 26, 1990

LEFT OFFICE:

John W. Dinkelspiel   November 27, 1990      November 30, 1990
